description_long: This is a standard Western-style toilet with a white ceramic body and an attached rectangular cistern at the back. The toilet seat and lid are both present, with the seat in an upright position, revealing a stained and slightly discolored bowl interior. The cistern features a visible flush lever on the left side, and the overall surface shows signs of wear and grime, particularly around the rim and bowl. The base is broad and flat, designed for stable floor mounting, and the tank is proportionally large to store sufficient water for flushing. The object is primarily constructed from glazed ceramic, giving it a hard, smooth, and slightly glossy finish. The design is typical for public or residential restrooms in urban environments.
description: A white ceramic floor-mounted toilet with a rectangular cistern, upright seat, and visible flush lever, showing signs of wear and staining.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open bowl, upright seat, and the full face of the cistern.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the bowl, the upright seat, and the flush lever on the cistern.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cistern and the top of the seat, with no visible flush mechanism.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the bowl profile and cistern, with the seat upright and no flush lever visible.
